content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Transfer your phone number

  - url: https://www.t-mobile.com/support/account/transfer-your-phone-number

  - source: T-Mobile Support

  - domain: t-mobile.com


  ## Content


  # Transfer your phone number


  ## Get a Temporary Port Out PIN


  For a personal account, a Temporary Port Out PIN is required to move the number
  from T-Mobile. Only the Primary Account Holder can generate this temporary PIN.


  T-Mobile provides the generation process through T-Life and through T-Mobile.com.


  ### Before trying to generate the PIN


  Use the T-Mobile device that has the relevant SIM or eSIM active. The request may
  need to be made while that device is connected to the T-Mobile network with Wi-Fi
  turned off.


  Private VPN services and similar privacy relays can interfere with the request and
  may need to be temporarily disabled.


  If the customer is not the Primary Account Holder, the support page directs that
  person to ask the Primary Account Holder to request the PIN.


  T-Mobile also provides an official Contact Us path for additional help with a transfer
  PIN.


  ### T-Life path for postpaid accounts


  1. Sign in to T-Life from the T-Mobile device while using the T-Mobile network.

  2. Open the account area and then the permissions and controls section.

  3. Select the transfer-PIN option.

  4. Complete any additional account verification that appears.

  5. Create the temporary PIN and record the value shown on screen.


  If the request is not made from the expected device or network context, the app
  may show an error and direct the customer to retry under the required conditions.


  ### T-Mobile.com path for postpaid accounts


  The web procedure is designed for a mobile browser on the customer''s T-Mobile device
  rather than a desktop computer.


  1. Sign in to the T-Mobile account from the mobile device.

  2. Open the profile area.

  3. Go to permissions and controls.

  4. Select the transfer-PIN option.

  5. Complete any additional verification requested.

  6. Create the PIN and record the value shown.


  The page also notes that Port Out Protection must be disabled on the line before
  the temporary port-out credential can be used.


  ### Prepaid accounts


  Prepaid customers use the profile area of their T-Mobile account and choose the
  transfer-PIN request option. If Port Out Protection is active, it must be turned
  off before the transfer credential can be used.


  ## Official fallback


  Customers who cannot complete the PIN process are directed to T-Mobile''s official
  support channels. The page provides Contact Us options and the customer-care number
  1-800-937-8997 for additional transfer-PIN assistance.

  '

title: Transfer your phone number

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Protect against phone number port-out scams

  - url: https://www.t-mobile.com/customers/phone-number-port-out-scam-alert

  - source: T-Mobile

  - domain: t-mobile.com


  ## Content


  # Protect against phone number port-out scams


  Phone-number port-out fraud is a problem across the wireless industry. A criminal
  may try to move a victim''s number to another wireless provider so that calls and
  text messages, including some login-security messages, are redirected to the criminal.


  T-Mobile recommends using Port Out Protection to reduce the risk of an unauthorized
  transfer. The feature is managed from T-Mobile account channels. A Primary Account
  Holder or an Authorized User can add the protection, while removal is restricted
  to the Primary Account Holder.


  Customers can manage the feature through the T-Mobile app or T-Mobile.com in the
  account''s add-on management area.


  ## How a standard number transfer is validated


  When a customer moves a number from T-Mobile to another wireless provider, the receiving
  wireless provider uses a carrier-to-carrier transfer process.


  As part of that process, the receiving provider must validate the transfer request
  with T-Mobile using the Port Out PIN issued for the request.


  The Port Out PIN is an additional transfer-security element. It does not replace
  the password used to sign in to a T-Mobile account. The receiving wireless provider
  needs the numeric port-out credential as part of the carrier transfer process.


  Customers should use carrier-controlled transfer channels and should not treat an
  unrelated support service as a substitute for the carrier validation process.


  ## Additional account-protection steps


  Use strong, unique passwords for online and mobile accounts. Customers who need
  help with a suspected port-out problem can contact T-Mobile Customer Care at 1-800-937-8997.


  For broader privacy and security information, T-Mobile directs customers to its
  privacy and online-safety resources.

  '

title: Protect against phone number port-out scams
url: https://www.t-mobile.com/customers/phone-number-port-out-scam-alert

I can help. I checked the transfer case and found:

- **Case:** TMO-PORT-10482  
- **Status:** waiting for T-Mobile carrier authentication  
- Ordinary prerequisites are complete.
- **Port Out Protection is already disabled**, so it isn’t blocking the transfer.
- The support page you found is **JustAnswer**, an independent, non-authorized service—not T-Mobile. Don’t provide it your transfer PIN, recovery code, password, one-time code, or payment information.

For a T-Mobile number transfer, the missing item is the **temporary Port Out PIN**. Only the Primary Account Holder can generate it. Using the T-Mobile device with that SIM/eSIM active:

1. Turn off Wi‑Fi and any VPN/privacy relay.
2. Open **T-Life** or T-Mobile.com on that device and sign in.
3. Go to **Profile → Permissions and controls → Transfer PIN**.
4. Complete T-Mobile’s verification and generate the temporary PIN.
5. Give the PIN **only to the receiving carrier** through its official porting process—not to me or a third-party support site.

If it fails, contact T-Mobile through its official Contact Us channel or call **1-800-937-8997**. Also, don’t share the recovery option here; use it only within T-Mobile’s official app/site.
